revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Use shortened footnotes/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Use shortened footnotes/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Use shortened footnotes/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Use shortened footnotes/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Use shortened footnotes/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Use shortened footnotes/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
== Usage ==
Place this template near the top of articles that use the [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] for their citation style.
Wikipedia articles that use shortened footnotes for their citation style, either because of the [[WP:RETAIN|first main contributor]] rule or closely related subject field, may be systematically tagged with {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}}. The template facilitates article maintenance by enabling [[WP:bots|bots]] to recognize use of this format and by adding the article to the [[WP:hidden category|hidden category]] [[:Category:Use shortened footnotes|Use shortened footnotes]]. The template is invisible except in edit mode.
This template should be applied only to articles that exclusively use shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations. It should not be applied to articles that use a mix of standard and shortened footnotes unless there is a consensus on the article's talk page that the article should be converted to shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations.
Use of this template is part of a continuing effort to monitor the citation formats used in articles, to assist in maintaining consistent formatting within an article. It is ''not'' a temporary "cleanup" template. Therefore, do not remove the template without valid reason, such as a determination the article uses or should use a different citation format.
After being tagged, and bearing in mind article evolution, a [[WP:bot]] can eventually be programmed to clean up formats periodically, correcting any new introductions since its last visit, and updating the visit date on the {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}} template. Note that no such bot exists at this point in time.
([[User:Ohconfucius/script/MOSNUM dates|MOSNUM script documentation]] may also be relevant to those interested.)
== Syntax ==
{{Tlx|Use shortened footnotes|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}
<!--== Redirects == * {{Tl|Use parenthetical referencing}}-->
== See also == <!--* {{Tl|Parenthetical referencing editnotice}}--> * {{Tl|Use list-defined references}} * {{cl|Use English templates}} (a similar set of templates, used to maintain [[WP:ENGVAR|the variety of English used within an article]])
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Templates with no visible output]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
== Usage ==
Place this template near the top of articles that use the [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] for their citation style.
Wikipedia articles that use shortened footnotes for their citation style, either because of the [[WP:RETAIN|first main contributor]] rule or closely related subject field, may be systematically tagged with {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}}. The template facilitates article maintenance by enabling [[WP:bots|bots]] to recognize use of this format and by adding the article to the [[WP:hidden category|hidden category]] [[:Category:Use shortened footnotes|Use shortened footnotes]]. The template is invisible except in edit mode.
This template should be applied only to articles that exclusively use shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations. It should not be applied to articles that use a mix of standard and shortened footnotes unless there is a consensus on the article's talk page that the article should be converted to shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations.
Use of this template is part of a continuing effort to monitor the citation formats used in articles, to assist in maintaining consistent formatting within an article. It is ''not'' a temporary "cleanup" template. Therefore, do not remove the template without valid reason, such as a determination the article uses or should use a different citation format.
After being tagged, and bearing in mind article evolution, a [[WP:bot]] can eventually be programmed to clean up formats periodically, correcting any new introductions since its last visit, and updating the visit date on the {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}} template. Note that no such bot exists at this point in time.
([[User:Ohconfucius/script/MOSNUM dates|MOSNUM script documentation]] may also be relevant to those interested.)
== Syntax ==
{{Tlx|Use shortened footnotes|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}
<!--== Redirects == * {{Tl|Use parenthetical referencing}}-->
== See also == <!--* {{Tl|Parenthetical referencing editnotice}}--> * {{Tl|Use list-defined references}} * {{cl|Use English templates}} (a similar set of templates, used to maintain [[WP:ENGVAR|the variety of English used within an article]])
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Templates with no visible output]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] }}</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
== Usage ==
Place this template near the top of articles that use the [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] for their citation style.
Wikipedia articles that use shortened footnotes for their citation style, either because of the [[WP:RETAIN|first main contributor]] rule or closely related subject field, may be systematically tagged with {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}}. The template facilitates article maintenance by enabling [[WP:bots|bots]] to recognize use of this format and by adding the article to the [[WP:hidden category|hidden category]] [[:Category:Use shortened footnotes|Use shortened footnotes]]. The template is invisible except in edit mode.
This template should be applied only to articles that exclusively use shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations. It should not be applied to articles that use a mix of standard and shortened footnotes unless there is a consensus on the article's talk page that the article should be converted to shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations.
Use of this template is part of a continuing effort to monitor the citation formats used in articles, to assist in maintaining consistent formatting within an article. It is ''not'' a temporary "cleanup" template. Therefore, do not remove the template without valid reason, such as a determination the article uses or should use a different citation format.
After being tagged, and bearing in mind article evolution, a [[WP:bot]] can eventually be programmed to clean up formats periodically, correcting any new introductions since its last visit, and updating the visit date on the {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}} template. Note that no such bot exists at this point in time.
([[User:Ohconfucius/script/MOSNUM dates|MOSNUM script documentation]] may also be relevant to those interested.)
== Syntax ==
{{Tlx|Use shortened footnotes|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}
<!--== Redirects == * {{Tl|Use parenthetical referencing}}-->
== See also == <!--* {{Tl|Parenthetical referencing editnotice}}--> * {{Tl|Use list-defined references}} * {{cl|Use English templates}} (a similar set of templates, used to maintain [[WP:ENGVAR|the variety of English used within an article]])
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Templates with no visible output]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
== Usage ==
Place this template near the top of articles that use the [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] for their citation style.
Wikipedia articles that use shortened footnotes for their citation style, either because of the [[WP:RETAIN|first main contributor]] rule or closely related subject field, may be systematically tagged with {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}}. The template facilitates article maintenance by enabling [[WP:bots|bots]] to recognize use of this format and by adding the article to the [[WP:hidden category|hidden category]] [[:Category:Use shortened footnotes|Use shortened footnotes]]. The template is invisible except in edit mode.
This template should be applied only to articles that exclusively use shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations. It should not be applied to articles that use a mix of standard and shortened footnotes unless there is a consensus on the article's talk page that the article should be converted to shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations.
Use of this template is part of a continuing effort to monitor the citation formats used in articles, to assist in maintaining consistent formatting within an article. It is ''not'' a temporary "cleanup" template. Therefore, do not remove the template without valid reason, such as a determination the article uses or should use a different citation format.
After being tagged, and bearing in mind article evolution, a [[WP:bot]] can eventually be programmed to clean up formats periodically, correcting any new introductions since its last visit, and updating the visit date on the {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}} template. Note that no such bot exists at this point in time.
([[User:Ohconfucius/script/MOSNUM dates|MOSNUM script documentation]] may also be relevant to those interested.)
== Syntax ==
{{Tlx|Use shortened footnotes|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}
<!--== Redirects == * {{Tl|Use parenthetical referencing}}-->
== See also == <!--* {{Tl|Parenthetical referencing editnotice}}--> * {{Tl|Use list-defined references}} * {{cl|Use English templates}} (a similar set of templates, used to maintain [[WP:ENGVAR|the variety of English used within an article]])
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Templates with no visible output]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] }}</includeonly> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23674194100|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
== Usage ==
Place this template near the top of articles that use the [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] for their citation style.
Wikipedia articles that use shortened footnotes for their citation style, either because of the [[WP:RETAIN|first main contributor]] rule or closely related subject field, may be systematically tagged with {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}}. The template facilitates article maintenance by enabling [[WP:bots|bots]] to recognize use of this format and by adding the article to the [[WP:hidden category|hidden category]] [[:Category:Use shortened footnotes|Use shortened footnotes]]. The template is invisible except in edit mode.
This template should be applied only to articles that exclusively use shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations. It should not be applied to articles that use a mix of standard and shortened footnotes unless there is a consensus on the article's talk page that the article should be converted to shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations.
Use of this template is part of a continuing effort to monitor the citation formats used in articles, to assist in maintaining consistent formatting within an article. It is ''not'' a temporary "cleanup" template. Therefore, do not remove the template without valid reason, such as a determination the article uses or should use a different citation format.
After being tagged, and bearing in mind article evolution, a [[WP:bot]] can eventually be programmed to clean up formats periodically, correcting any new introductions since its last visit, and updating the visit date on the {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}} template. Note that no such bot exists at this point in time.
([[User:Ohconfucius/script/MOSNUM dates|MOSNUM script documentation]] may also be relevant to those interested.)
== Syntax ==
{{Tlx|Use shortened footnotes|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}
<!--== Redirects == * {{Tl|Use parenthetical referencing}}-->
== See also == <!--* {{Tl|Parenthetical referencing editnotice}}--> * {{Tl|Use list-defined references}} * {{cl|Use English templates}} (a similar set of templates, used to maintain [[WP:ENGVAR|the variety of English used within an article]])

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}}
== Usage ==
Place this template near the top of articles that use the [[Help:Shortened footnotes|shortened footnotes]] for their citation style.
Wikipedia articles that use shortened footnotes for their citation style, either because of the [[WP:RETAIN|first main contributor]] rule or closely related subject field, may be systematically tagged with {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}}. The template facilitates article maintenance by enabling [[WP:bots|bots]] to recognize use of this format and by adding the article to the [[WP:hidden category|hidden category]] [[:Category:Use shortened footnotes|Use shortened footnotes]]. The template is invisible except in edit mode.
This template should be applied only to articles that exclusively use shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations. It should not be applied to articles that use a mix of standard and shortened footnotes unless there is a consensus on the article's talk page that the article should be converted to shortened footnotes with a separate list of full citations.
Use of this template is part of a continuing effort to monitor the citation formats used in articles, to assist in maintaining consistent formatting within an article. It is ''not'' a temporary "cleanup" template. Therefore, do not remove the template without valid reason, such as a determination the article uses or should use a different citation format.
After being tagged, and bearing in mind article evolution, a [[WP:bot]] can eventually be programmed to clean up formats periodically, correcting any new introductions since its last visit, and updating the visit date on the {{Tl|Use shortened footnotes}} template. Note that no such bot exists at this point in time.
([[User:Ohconfucius/script/MOSNUM dates|MOSNUM script documentation]] may also be relevant to those interested.)
== Syntax ==
{{Tlx|Use shortened footnotes|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}}
== See also ==
* {{Tl|Use list-defined references}} * {{cl|Use English templates}} (a similar set of templates, used to maintain [[WP:ENGVAR|the variety of English used within an article]])
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other||
}}</includeonly> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates with no visible output]] [[Category:Citation and verifiability maintenance templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
